articles.title: Escaping the 47°C Telangana Heat: Where Hyderabadis Are Heading This Weekend - NativePlanet
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a red alert for Telangana until May 21. Temperatures are hitting 44 to 47 degrees Celsius across the state. This intense heat is driving Hyderabad residents to plan quick weekend escapes. Many families are searching for cooler spots to find relief from the sun.
The IMD expects extreme heat to persist through the next week. Travel experts report a massive surge in bookings for local hill stations. Most tourists want to avoid the peak afternoon sun. They are choosing destinations that offer a slight drop in temperature and lush shade.
Popular cooler escapes near Hyderabad during the heatwave
Ananthagiri Hills stands as the most popular choice for a day trip. It is about 90 kilometres away from the state capital. The dense forests of Srisailam also provide a natural canopy for travellers. These locations offer a needed break from the urban heat island effect.
For those seeking a longer trip, Horsley Hills is drawing many weekend visitors. This hill station requires an overnight stay due to the driving distance. It remains significantly cooler than the plains during these peak summer months. Local resorts in the area are reporting very high occupancy levels lately.
Safety tips for weekend road trips from Hyderabad
Safety is vital when travelling during a severe heatwave. Experts advise starting all road trips before the sun rises. Pre-dawn starts help drivers avoid the most dangerous noon heat. Keeping the vehicle well-maintained and staying hydrated are essential steps for tourists.
Following these simple travel tips ensures a safe and comfortable journey. Exploring these nearby gems provides a mental and physical break from the heat. As the IMD alert continues, planning ahead remains the best way to stay cool. Enjoy the serene hills while staying safe and hydrated this summer.

articles.title: Severe Heatwave Alert Issued Across Telangana Until May 21 - The South India Times
articles.full_text:
Authorities and weather observers have warned people across Telangana to prepare for extremely hot conditions over the coming days as temperatures continue to rise sharply in many regions of the state.
According to forecasts, several districts marked under high risk zones are likely to experience severe heatwave conditions starting from May 14. Northern and eastern parts of Telangana are expected to face the highest impact, with temperatures in some locations possibly climbing close to 47 degrees Celsius.
Residents have been advised to remain cautious during afternoon hours as the risk of heatstroke is expected to increase significantly. People are being encouraged to avoid unnecessary travel under direct sunlight and stay hydrated during the peak heat period.
Hyderabad is also likely to witness uncomfortable weather conditions during the heatwave spell. Temperature levels in the city are expected to remain between 42 and 44 degrees Celsius for several days, increasing discomfort for residents.
Health experts have urged elderly people, children, and outdoor workers to take extra precautions as prolonged exposure to extreme heat may lead to dehydration and other health complications. Officials are continuing to monitor weather conditions closely across the state.
